title: Food Coloring on Carpet
stainChemistry: Food coloring is concentrated synthetic dye (Red 40, Blue 1, Yellow 5 / Tartrazine — FDA-approved azo / triphenylmethane dyes) in water + glycerin carrier. Engineered to bond to fabric / cellulose dramatically through grip — same mechanism as deliberate dye application. On carpet, fresh food coloring rinses with cold water + Dawn. Set / hot-dried food coloring is often permanent. Red 40 is the hardest variant. NO chlorine bleach panic moves — bleach can lighten food dye on white synthetic carpet but strips carpet dye AND damages fiber on most synthetic carpet. Safer path: Dawn + 3% peroxide (whites) + sun, with reducing-bleach (Rit Color Remover) as nuclear option for white-only carpet.
whyThisWorks: Cold water + Dawn lifts fresh undeveloped dye before it bonds permanently. 3% peroxide oxidizes azo / triphenylmethane color on white carpet. Sunlight (UV) photodegrades Red 40. Rit Color Remover (sodium hydrosulfite) is the home nuclear option for whites — strips ALL dye, so colored-carpet bans. Speed + escalation discipline matter most. Heat is the hard line — hot dryer / hot extractor sets food coloring permanently.
defaultAssumption: Kid baking / crafts / Easter eggs / homemade playdough on living-room carpet. Fresh = high-confidence DIY. Dried-after-dryer = often permanent or accept-as-is.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Dampen cloth with COLD water. Press on stain, lift, rotate, repeat. Cold only — heat sets food coloring permanently. Never pour.
Dawn cold water on damp cloth. Soft brush. Damp-cloth rinse — never pour. 2–3 cycles for concentrated colors (Red 40 is the toughest).
WHITE carpet only: 3% peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min. Hidden test on dyed carpet first — peroxide can lighten carpet dye.
Open blinds so daylight falls on the spot for several hours. UV breaks down Red 40 + Blue 1 gradually. Multi-day if needed.

spottingProtocol.agent: Rit Color Remover (WHITE Carpet Only, Nuclear Option)
spottingProtocol.equipment: Rit Color Remover per label, ventilation
spottingProtocol.instruction: WHITE carpet only. NEVER on colored / dyed carpet — strips carpet dye too. Per label, ventilation required (sulfur smell). Skip on synthetic carpet that may be heat-sensitive — Rit needs warm water in some formulations.
Weighted paper towels overnight. Fan-dry. Inspect in daylight. Air-dry — NO hot extractor / steam — heat sets food coloring permanently.

steps: 2. Dawn + soft toothbrush, 5 min cold, 2–3 cycles.
3. 3% H₂O₂ damp cloth (white carpet only), 5–10 min.
5. Rit Color Remover (white carpet only, sodium hydrosulfite, ventilation).
products: Rit Color Remover (white carpet only, nuclear option)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — heat sets food coloring permanently
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets food coloring permanently
NEVER hot dryer / hot extractor / steam — heat = permanent
NEVER chlorine bleach panic moves — strips carpet dye, damages synthetic fiber
Rit Color Remover = WHITE carpet only (strips ALL dye)
Hidden-area peroxide test on dyed carpet

homeSolutions.agent: Speed Matters — Heat Sets It Forever
homeSolutions.instruction: Food coloring is concentrated dye engineered to BOND. Caught fresh = high success. Set or hot-dried = often permanent. Two rules: NO hot water (sets the dye), NO chlorine bleach panic moves (strips carpet dye + damages synthetic fiber). The safe escalation ladder: cold water → Dawn → 3% peroxide on white only → sun → Rit Color Remover (white-carpet nuclear option only).

Dampen a clean white cloth with COLD water. Press firmly on the spot, lift, rotate to a clean section, press again. Cold ONLY — anything warmer than cold sets food coloring permanently. NEVER pour water on the carpet.

Mix 1 tablespoon Dawn in 2 cups cold water. Dampen a cloth, work into the spot with a soft toothbrush 5 minutes. Damp-cloth rinse. Repeat 2–3 cycles for concentrated colors. Red 40 is the toughest — budget extra rounds.
WHITE carpet only: dab 3% drugstore peroxide on a damp cloth, press 5–10 minutes. SKIP on dyed carpet — peroxide can lighten carpet dye. Hidden-area test first if uncertain.
Open blinds so daylight falls on the spot for several hours. UV breaks down Red 40 + Blue 1 gradually. Multi-day exposure if needed. Movable area rugs can go outside in sun.

homeSolutions.agent: Rit Color Remover — Last Resort for WHITE Carpet ONLY
homeSolutions.instruction: If WHITE carpet still has color after sun + peroxide, Rit Color Remover (sodium hydrosulfite) is the home nuclear option. Per label. Ventilate (sulfur smell). DO NOT use on colored carpet — strips ALL dye including the carpet's. Skip if you can't confirm white carpet.
Stack paper towels on the spot, weight with a heavy book, leave overnight. Fan-dry in the morning. NO hot extractor / steam — heat sets food coloring permanently.

steps: 1. NO hot water. NO chlorine bleach panic moves.
3. Dawn + cold water + soft toothbrush, 2–3 cycles.
4. 3% peroxide damp cloth (WHITE carpet only), 5–10 min.
6. Rit Color Remover (WHITE carpet only, nuclear option, ventilate).
7. Weighted paper towels overnight + AIR-DRY. No hot extractor.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot water / hot dryer was used (likely permanent), if Rit Color Remover would strip carpet dye (colored carpet), or after 3 home cycles + sunlight without progress.
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets food coloring permanently
NEVER hot dryer / hot extractor / steam — heat permanent
NEVER chlorine bleach — strips carpet dye + damages fiber
Rit Color Remover = WHITE carpet ONLY
Speed matters more than chemistry choice

neverDo: Never hot water on food coloring
Never hot dryer / hot extractor / steam
Never Rit Color Remover on colored carpet
fiberSensitivities: Nylon / polyester / olefin synthetic carpet: tolerates Dawn + 3% peroxide (white)
Wool carpet: pro-only for food coloring (peroxide yellows wool)
Dyed carpet: hidden peroxide test mandatory
Heat sets food coloring permanently across all fibers

materialWarnings: No chlorine bleach panic moves on carpet
Rit Color Remover is white-only nuclear option
Speed matters more than chemistry choice

escalation.when: Hot water / dryer used previously, Rit Color Remover would strip carpet dye, 3 home cycles + sunlight plateau, or wool carpet (always).
escalation.whatToTell: "Food coloring on [white / colored / wool] carpet, color [Red 40 / Blue 1 / Yellow 5 / mixed], age [fresh / hot-dried / older], home-treated with cold + Dawn × 2–3 + [3% peroxide on white] + sunlight + [Rit Color Remover white-only]. No hot water / no chlorine bleach. [Residual color / hot-dried suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional carpet cleaner with reducing-agent chemistry (sodium hydrosulfite at controlled dilution)

contextQuestions: How long ago — fresh / overnight / hot-dried?
Has hot extractor / steam been used?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Red 40 is the toughest variant — budget extra rounds. Yellow 5 (Tartrazine) easier. Hot-dried = often permanent. Wool carpet + food coloring = pro from minute one (peroxide yellows wool, Rit damages keratin).
